title: ageratum corymbosum f. elachycarpum
intro: Ageratum corymbosum f. elachycarpum, also known as Blue Mule's Ears, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Ageratum corymbosum f. elachycarpum include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant is prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat these conditions.
